author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XCVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My lord and brother Severus still defers the pleasure we shall feel in his discourse, which he oweth us; for he acknowledgeth, that he is held a debtor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all the Churches through which he hath passed, by his tongue the lord hath gladdened: much more therefore ought that Church to be rejoiced, out of which the Lord hath propagated his preaching among the rest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what shall we do, but obey his will?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I said, however, brethren, that he deferred, not that he defrauded us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore let us keep him as a debtor bound, and release him not until he hath paid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend therefore, beloved: as far as the Lord alloweth, let us say somewhat of this Psalm, which indeed you already know; for the fresh mention of truth is sweet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Possibly when its title was pronounced, some heard it with wonder.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Psalm is inscribed: “When the house was being built after the Captivity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This title having been prefixed, ye were perhaps expecting in the text of the Psalm to hear what stones were hewn from the mountains, what masses were drawn to the spot, what foundations were laid, what beams were placed on high, what columns raised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Its song is of nothing of this kind.…It is no such house that is in building; for behold where it is built, not in one spot, not in any particular region.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus he beginneth:—

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O sing unto the Lord a new song; sing unto the Lord, all the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If all the earth singeth a new song, it is thus building while it singeth: the very act of singing is building: but only, if it singeth not the old song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The lust of the flesh singeth the old song: the love of God singeth the new.…Hear why it is a new song: the Lord saith, “A new commandment I give unto you, that ye love one another.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole earth then singeth a new song: there the house of God is built.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the earth is the house of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If all the earth is the house of God, he who clingeth not to all the earth, is a ruin, not a house; that old ruin whose shadow that ancient temple represented.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there what was old was destroyed, that what was new might be built up.…The Apostle bindeth us together into this very structure, and fasteneth us when bound together in that unity, saying, “Forbearing one another in love; endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where there is this unity of Spirit, there is one stone; but one stone formed out of many.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How one formed out of many?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By forbearing one another in love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the house of the Lord our God is in building; it is this that is being wrought, for this are these words, for this these readings, for this the preaching of the Gospel over the whole world; as yet it is in building.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This house hath increased greatly, and filled many nations: nevertheless, it hath not yet prevailed through all nations: by its increase it hath held many, and will prevail over all: and it is gainsaid by those who boast of their being of its household, and who say, it hath already lost ground.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It still increaseth, still all those nations which have not yet believed are destined to believe; that no man may say, will that tongue believe? will the barbarians believe? what is the meaning of the Holy Spirit having appeared in the fiery tongues, except that there is no tongue so hard that it cannot be softened by that fire?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we know that many barbarous nations have already believed in Christ: Christ already possesseth regions where the Roman empire hath never yet reached; what is as yet closed to those who fight with the sword, is not closed to Him who fighteth with wood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the Lord hath reigned from the wood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is it who fighteth with wood?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With His cross He hath vanquished kings, and fixed upon their forehead, when vanquished, that very cross; and they glory in it, for in it is their salvation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the work which is being wrought, thus the house increaseth, thus it is building: and that ye may know, hear the following verses of the Psalm: see them labouring upon, and constructing the house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O sing unto the Lord all the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing unto the Lord, bless His Name: be telling good tidings of His salvation from day to day” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth the building increase?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be telling,” he saith, “good tidings of His salvation from day to day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it be preached from day to day; from day to day, he saith, let it be built; let My house, saith God, increase.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as if it were said by the workmen, Where dost Thou command it to be built?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where dost Thou will Thy house to increase?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Choose for us some level, spacious spot, if Thou wish an ample house built Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where dost Thou bid us be telling good tidings from day to day?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He showeth the place: “Declare His honour unto the heathen:” His honour, not yours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O ye builders, “Declare His honour unto the heathen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Should ye choose to declare your own honour, ye shall fall: if His, ye shall be built up, while ye are building.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore they who choose to declare their own honour, have refused to dwell in that house; and therefore they sing not a new song with all the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they do not share it with the whole round world; and hence they are not building in the house, but have erected a whited wall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How sternly doth God threaten the whited wall?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are innumerable testimonies of the Prophets, whence He curseth the whited wall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the whited wall, save hypocrisy, that is, pretence?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Without it is bright, within it is dirt.…A certain person, speaking of this whited wall, said thus: “as, if in a wall which standeth alone, and is not connected with any other walls, you make a door, whoever enters, is out of doors; so in that part which hath refused to sing the new song together with the house, but hath chosen to build a wall, and that a whited one, and not solid, what availeth it that it hath a door?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou enterest, thou art found to be without.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For because they themselves did not enter by the door, their door also doth not admit them within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord saith, “I am the door: by Me they enter in.”…“Declare His honour unto the heathen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, unto the heathen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps by nations but a few are meant: and that part which hath raised the whited wall hath still somewhat to say: why are not Getulia, Numidia, Mauritania, Byzacium, nations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Provinces are nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the word of God take the word from hypocrisy, from the whited wall, building up the house over the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not enough to say, “Declare His honour unto the heathen;” that thou mayest not think any nations excepted, he addeth, “and His wonders unto all people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord is great, and cannot worthily be praised” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What Lord, except Jesus Christ, “is great, and cannot worthily be praised”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know surely that He appeared as a Man: ye know surely that He was conceived in a woman’s womb, ye know that He was born from the womb, that He was suckled, that He was carried in arms, circumcised, that a victim was offered for Him, that He grew; lastly, ye know that He was buffeted, spit upon, crowned with thorns, was crucified, died, was pierced with a spear; ye know that He suffered all these things: “He is great, and cannot worthily be praised.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Despise not what is little, understand what is great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He became little, because ye were such: let Him be acknowledged great, and in Him ye shall be great.…For what can a small tongue say towards the praise of the Great One?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By saying, Beyond praise, he hath spoken, and hath given to imagination what it may conceive: as if saying, What I cannot utter, do thou reflect on; and when thou shalt have reflected, it will not be enough.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What no man’s thought uttereth, doth any man’s tongue utter?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is great, and cannot worthily be praised.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him be praised, and preached: His honour declared, and His house built.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5. …For the spot where he wished to build the house, is itself woody, where it was said yesterday, “we found it in the wood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he was seeking that very house, when he said, “in the wood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And why is that spot woody?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men used to worship images: it is not wonderful that they fed hogs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that son who left his father, and spent his all on harlots, living as a prodigal, used to feed hogs, that is, to worship devils; and by this very superstition of the heathen, all the earth became a wood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he who buildeth a house, rooteth up the wood; and for this reason it was said, “While the house was being built, after the captivity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For men were held captive under the devil, and served devils; but they were redeemed from captivity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They could sell, but they could not redeem themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Redeemer came, and gave a price; He poured forth His Blood, and bought the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye ask what He bought?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see what He hath given; find out then what He bought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Blood of Christ was the price.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is equal to this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, but the whole world?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, but all nations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are very ungrateful for their price, or very proud, who say that the price is so small that it bought the Africans only; or that they are so great, as that it was given for them alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them not then exult, let them not be proud: He gave what He gave for the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knew what He bought, because He knew at what price He bought it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus because we are redeemed, the house is built after the captivity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who are they who held us in captivity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they to whom it is said, “Declare His honour,” are the clearers of the wood: that they may root out the wood, free the earth from captivity, and build, and raise up, by declaring the greatness of the Lord’s house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is the wood of devils cleared away, unless He who is above them all be preached?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All nations then had devils for their gods: those whom they called gods, were devils, as the Apostle more openly saith, “The things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice unto devils, and not to God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since therefore they were in captivity, because they sacrificed to devils, and on that account the whole earth had remained woody; He is declared to be great, and above all worldly praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6. …For when he had said, “He is more to be feared than all gods:” he added, “As for all the gods of the heathen, they are devils.”…Because “all the gods of the heathen are devils.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And is this all the praise of Him who cannot worthily be praised, that He is above all the gods of the heathen, which are devils?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wait, and hear what followeth: “It is the Lord that made the heavens.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not above all gods only therefore, but above all the heavens which He made, is the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he were to say, “above all gods, for the gods of the heathen are devils,” and if the praise of our Lord stopped here, he had said less than we are accustomed to think of Christ; but when he said, “But it is the Lord that made the heavens;” see what difference there is between the heavens and devils: and what between the heavens and Him who made the heavens; behold how exalted is the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, But the Lord sitteth above the heavens; for perhaps some one else might be imagined to have made them, upon which He was enthroned: but, “It is the Lord that made the heavens.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He made the heavens, He made the Angels also: Himself made the Angels, Himself made the Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devils yielded to the Apostles: but the Apostles themselves were heavens, who bore the Lord.…O heavens, which He made, declare His honour unto the heathen!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let His house be built throughout the earth, let all the earth sing a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Confession and beauty are before Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou love beauty?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wishest thou to be beautiful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, beauty and confession, but confession and beauty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wast foul; confess, that thou mayest be fair: thou wast a sinner; confess, that thou mayest be righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou couldest deform thyself: thou canst not make thyself beautiful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But of what sort is our Betrothed, who hath loved one deformed, that he might make her fair?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, saith some one, loved He one deformed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I came not,” said He, “to call the righteous, but sinners.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom callest Thou? sinners, that they may remain sinners?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, saith He.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And by what means will they cease to be sinners?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Confession and beauty are before Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They honour Him by confession of their sins, they vomit the evils which they had greedily devoured; they return not to their vomit, like the unclean dog; and there will then be confession and beauty: we love beauty; let us first choose confession, that beauty may follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, there is one who loveth power and greatness: he wisheth to be great as the Angels are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a certain greatness in the Angels; and such power, that if the Angels exert it to the full, it cannot be withstood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And every man desireth the power of the Angels, but their righteousness every man loveth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First love righteousness, and power shall follow thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what followeth here?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Holiness and greatness are in His sanctification.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wast before seeking for greatness: first love righteousness: when thou art righteous, thou shall also be great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou preposterously dost wish first to be great, thou fallest before thou canst rise: for thou dost not rise, thou art raised up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou risest better, if He raise thee who falleth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He who falleth not descendeth unto thee: thou hadst fallen: He descendeth, He hath stretched forth His hand unto thee; thou canst not rise by thy own strength, embrace the hand of Him who descendeth, that thou mayest be raised up by the Strong One.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If “confession and beauty are before Him: holiness and greatness in His sanctification” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This we declare, when we are building the house; behold, it is already declared unto the heathen; what ought the heathen to do, to whom those who have cleared away the wood have declared the Lord’s honour?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He now saith to the heathen themselves, “Ascribe unto the Lord, O ye kindreds of the people: ascribe unto the Lord worship and honour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ascribe them not unto yourselves: because they also who have declared it unto you, have not declared their own, but His honour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye then “ascribe unto the Lord worship and honour;” and say, “Not unto us, O Lord, not unto us: but unto Thy Name give the praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Put not your trust in man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If each of you is baptized, let him say: He baptizeth me, of whom the friend of the Bridegroom said, “He baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when ye say this, ye ascribe unto the Lord worship and honour: “Ascribe unto the Lord worship and honour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Ascribe unto the Lord glory unto His Name” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not unto the name of man, not unto your own name, but unto His ascribe worship.…Confession is a present unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O heathen, if ye will enter into His courts, enter not empty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bring presents.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What presents shall we bring with us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, “O God, shalt not Thou despise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Enter with an humble heart into the house of God, and thou hast entered with a present.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou art proud, thou enterest empty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whence wouldest thou be proud, if thou wert not empty?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou wast full, thou wouldest not be puffed up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How couldest thou be full?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou wert to bring a present, which thou shouldest carry to the courts of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not retain you much longer: let us run over what remaineth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the house increasing: behold the edifice pervade the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rejoice, because ye have entered into the courts; rejoice, because ye are being built into the temple of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those who enter are themselves built up, they themselves are the house of God: He is the inhabitor, for whom the house is built over the whole world, and this “after the captivity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bring presents, and come into His courts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O worship the Lord in His holy court” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9): in the Catholic Church; this is His holy court.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man say, “Lo, here is Christ, or there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there shall arise false prophets.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say this unto them, “There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye are calling me to the whited wall; I adore my God in His holy court.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the whole earth be moved before His face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Tell it out among the nations, that the Lord reigneth from the wood: and that it is He who hath made the round world so fast that it cannot be moved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What testimonies of the building of the house of God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The clouds of heaven thunder out throughout the world that God’s house is being built; and the frogs cry from the marsh, We alone are Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What testimonies do I bring forward?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That of the Psalter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I bring forward what thou singest as one deaf: open thine ears; thou singest this; thou singest with me, and thou agreest not with me; thy tongue soundeth what mine doth, and yet thine heart disagreeth with mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou not sing this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the testimonies of the whole world: “Let the whole earth be moved before His face:” and dost thou say, that thou art not moved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Tell it out among the heathen, that the Lord hath reigned from the wood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall men perchance prevail here, and say they reign by wood, because they reign by means of the clubs of their bandits?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reign by the Cross of Christ, if thou art to reign by wood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this wood of thine maketh thee wooden: the wood of Christ passeth thee across the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hearest the Psalm saying, “He hath set aright the round world, that it cannot be moved;” and thou sayest it hath not only been moved since it was made fast, but hath also decreased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou speak the truth, and the Psalmist falsehood?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do the false prophets, when they cry out, “Lo, here is Christ, and there,” speak truth; and doth this Prophet lie?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, against these most open words ye hear in the corners rumours like these; “such an one was a traditor,” and, “such an one was a traditor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dost thou say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are thy words, or the words of God, to be heard?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “it is He who hath set aright the round world, that it cannot be moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I show unto thee the round world built: bring thy present, and come into the courts of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast no presents: and on that account thou art not willing to enter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If God were to appoint unto thee a bull, goat, or ram, for a present, thou wouldest find one to bring: He hath appointed a humble heart, and thou wilt not enter; for thou findest not this in thyself, because thou art swollen with pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath set aright the round world, that it cannot be moved: and He shall judge the people righteously.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then shall they mourn, who now refuse to love righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the heavens, which declare the glory of God, rejoice; let the heavens rejoice, which the Lord made; let the earth be glad, which the heavens rain upon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the heavens are the preachers, the earth the listeners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the sea be stirred up, and the fulness thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sea hath been stirred up, and the fulness thereof: the whole world was roused up against the Church, while it was being extended and built over all the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Concerning this stirring up, ye have heard in the Gospel, “They shall deliver you up to councils.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The sea was stirred up: but how should the sea ever conquer Him who made it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The plains shall be joyful, and all things that are in them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the meek, all the gentle, all the righteous, are the “plains” of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then shall all the trees of the woods rejoice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The trees of the woods are the heathen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do they rejoice?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they were cut off from the wild olive, and engraffed into the good olive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then shall all the trees of the woods rejoice:” because huge cedars and cypresses have been cut down, and undecaying timbers have been bought for the building of the house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were trees of the woods; but before they were sent to the building: they were trees of the woods, but before they produced the olive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Before the face of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He cometh, for He cometh to judge the world” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came at first, and will come again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He first came in His Church in clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the clouds which bore Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostles who preached, respecting whom ye have heard, when the Epistle was being read: “We are ambassadors,” he saith, “for Christ: we pray you in Christ’s stead, be ye reconciled to God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the clouds in whom He cometh, excepting His last Advent, when He will come to judge the quick and the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came first in the clouds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was His first voice which sounded forth in the Gospel: “From this time shall they see the Son of Man coming in the clouds.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “from this time”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will not the Lord come in later times, when all the tribes of the earth shall mourn?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He first came in His own preachers, and filled the whole round world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not resist His first coming, that we may not tremble at His second.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But woe to them that are with child, and that give suck in those days!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard but now in the Gospel: “Take ye heed, for ye know not at what hour He cometh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is said figuratively.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are those with child, and who give suck?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who are with child, are the souls whose hope is in the world: but those who have gained what they hoped for, are meant by “they who give suck.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For example: one wisheth to buy a country seat; he is with child, for his object is not gained as yet, the womb swelleth in hope: he buyeth it; he hath brought forth, he now giveth suck to what he hath bought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Woe to them that are with child, and that give suck in those days!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Woe to those who put their hope in the world; woe to them that cling to those things which they brought forth through hope in the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then should the Christian do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He should use, not serve, the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those that have as those that have not.…He who is without carefulness, waiteth without fear for his Lord’s coming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what sort of love is it of Christ, to fear lest He come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, are we not ashamed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We love Him, and yet we fear lest He come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are we sure that we love Him? or do we love our sins more?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore let us hate our sins for their own sake, and love Him who will come to punish our sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will come, whether we like or not: for because He cometh not just now, it is no reason that He will not come at all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will come, and when thou knowest not; and if He shall find thee ready, thy ignorance is no hurt to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Then shall all the trees of the wood rejoice before the Lord; for He cometh:” at His first coming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what afterwards?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He cometh to judge the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And all the trees of the woods shall rejoice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came first: and later to judge the earth: He shall find those rejoicing who believed in His first coming, “for He cometh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For with righteousness shall He judge the world:” not a part of it, for He bought not a part: He will judge the whole, for it was the whole of which He paid the price.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard the Gospel, where it saith, that when He cometh, “He shall gather together His elect from the four winds.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He gathereth all His elect from the four winds: therefore from the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Adam himself (this I had said before) signifieth in Greek the whole world; for there are four letters, A, D, A, and M.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as the Greeks speak, the four quarters of the world have these initial letters, ’Ανατολὴ, they call the East; Δύσις, the West; ῎Αρκτος, the North; Μεσημβρία, the South: thou hast the word Adam.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Adam therefore hath been scattered over the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was in one place, and fell, and as in a manner broken small, he filled the whole world: but the mercy of God gathered together the fragments from every side, and forged them by the fire of love, and made one what was broken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Artist knew how to do this; let no one despair: it is indeed a great thing, but reflect who that Artist was.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who made, restored: He who formed, reformed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are righteousness and truth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will gather together His elect with Him to the judgment, but the rest He will separate one from another; for He will place some on the right, others on the left hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is more just, what more true, than that they shall not expect mercy from their Judge, who have refused to act mercifully, before their Judge come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those who chose to act with mercy, with mercy shall be judged.…
